shortBlurb: This stoneware jug is a turn-of-the-century piece, sporting that classic crackle finish. It's got an old handle, giving it some real character.

billsNote: Antique 233 / Apothecary Jar / Chemists Jar / Dark Cobalt Glass / $160.00
shortBlurb: This is a hefty piece, a big dark cobalt glass jar that served its time in an old apothecary or chemist shop. The color on this one, deep and mysterious, tells you it's got a story locked inside.
This old Pepsi crate is packed with a killer collection of embossed glass bottles. Each one's got its own story, from old remedies to flavoring extracts, ready for a new home.

billsNote: 1940-1960s / Pattern: Wildrose / By Indiana Glass Co. / $20
shortBlurb: This isn't just any old pressed glass, this is a beautiful lidded dish in the classic Wildrose pattern from the Indiana Glass Co., straight outta the mid-century. It's got that nice amber glow and makes a fine little container for your treasures.
Here's a couple of classic Star Wars characters, C-3PO and Master Yoda, from the 80s. These are original Kenner pieces, ready for another generation of action.

itemName: Mickey Mouse Donald Duck Rubber Figures
billsNote: Ves 1970s / Mickey Mouse / Donald Duck / Rubber Figures / $20.00
shortBlurb: These classic Mickey and Donald rubber figures are straight out of the 70s. You've got Mickey in his iconic red shirt and Donald looking dapper in his blue sailor suit. Real fun pieces for any Disney collector.

You're looking at a real Hubley Kiddie diecast race car from the 30s or 40s. It's got that classic worn green finish, ready to take to the track. I added a couple of plastic army men in and around it, just to give it some extra character, you know?

This little duckling bank is a real charmer, looks like it's ready to guard some treasure. And right next to it, a classic tin spinning top, takes you right back to simpler times with that great litho artwork.

displayName: Pre-War Teddy at the School Desk
shortBlurb: This big ol' Pre-War Teddy has seen some things, but he's still got that classic charm. Just look at him, he practically owns that school desk, ready for his next adventure.
This Steiff UHU Owl is a real classic, with that fluffy grey and white plumage and bright yellow eyes. A nice piece to add some character to your collection.

This old Pepsi crate is a real looker, got that classic red script and 'Drink Pepsi-Cola' right on the side. You can still see the '16' on the end, probably held a full case. Perfect for stashing your stuff or just for show, a solid piece of Americana from back in the day.
This is a classic circular enamel sign for Krueger Beer-Ale, a real eye-catcher with its bold red and white colors. Perfect for your vintage bar setup.
Here's a piece of history, a genuine Shasta Bitters wooden crate, still holding twelve vintage bottles. This old timber box has been around, you can tell by the rustic look of the wood.

displayName: Oak Park Train Station Tin Building
itemName: Oak Park Train Station Tin Building

shortBlurb: This is a real heavy-duty belt buckle, a tribute to America's firefighters. It's got everything: a classic horse-drawn pumper, a modern fire truck, and the Fire Dept. USA emblem, all set against a sharp red background. No doubt about it, they're "EVER READY, EVER WILLING."

billsNote: 1934 Ludwig / Scherk Renaissance / Perfume bottle / w/ Marie Antoinette / Picture / $225.00
shortBlurb: This isn't just a bottle, it's a 1934 Ludwig Scherk Renaissance perfume bottle. You're looking at ornate metalwork, those pretty pink cabochons, and a clear Marie Antoinette portrait right there in the frame.

billsNote: 1930s / General Electric / Cast-iron oscillating fan / Brass blades / Ask in person
shortBlurb: This ain't no plastic piece of junk. This is a real GE cast-iron oscillating fan, brass blades, original cord — built to last and keep you cool, just like they used to make 'em.

billsNote: Antique / Ind Farm Pully / Rodded wheel / # 60.00
shortBlurb: This here's an antique industrial farm pulley. Built tough with those rodded wheels, still ready to get some work done.

billsNote: W.G. 1970's / Ganie phone / Leather pouch / $175.00 / -- AND -- / 1940 / Bell sys. phone / (red) / $135.00
shortBlurb: Got some serious communication history here. A classic red Bell System rotary from '40, still got that original punch. And for something a little different, this Ganie phone in a cool leather pouch from the 70s—a real unique piece.
This is a real deal 1930s Brookins oil can, ready for some heavy-duty work. It's got that classic 1 1/4 gallon capacity, built tough with a galvanized finish and a sturdy handle.

maker: The Hall-Borchert Dress Form Co. New York City
billsNote: 1900's / Adjustable Dress / Form / New York City / $175.00
shortBlurb: This is a real early 1900s adjustable dress form, perfect for any serious collector or if you just want to display your best pieces. Look at that New York City pedigree on it. It’s got a great patina, still solid, and dressed up with a nice lace collar.

billsNote: Cloth Dolls / Raggedy Anns + Mammy doll / Hand-stitched / Ask in person
shortBlurb: A whole shelf of hand-stitched cloth dolls — Raggedy Anns with yarn-red hair, a Mammy doll in a printed-cotton dress with apron. Each one's seen a lot of love.
This little fellow is a genuine Steiff, a sweet baby lion with his original yellow bib. This vintage piece from the 1950s is still looking sharp and ready for a new home.

billsNote: 1920-1940 / Celluloid "The Washington / D.C." baby doll / Japan / Some paint missing / $30.00
shortBlurb: This celluloid baby doll, straight from Japan, it's a genuine 'Washington D.C.' piece from the twenties to the forties. Sure, it's got some paint missing, but that's just character, tells you it's seen some action.
